articles.title: US stocks and crude oil prices drift to a quiet finish of a bumpy week
articles.description: U.S. stocks and oil prices are drifting toward a quiet finish of the week following fireworks earlier on worries about how the war with Iran will affect the global flow of crude. The S&P 500 rose 0.1% early Friday and was on track to close out its fourth winning week in the last five. The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 140 points, and the Nasdaq composite was up 0.1%. Oil prices were holding relatively steady, even after a series of unclaimed airstrikes hit Iran after the U.S. said it finished its attacks. The price for a barrel of Brent crude rose 0.2%.

articles.title: India's 10-year bond logs best day in over a week on oil relief
articles.description: On Friday, Indian government bonds experienced a positive shift with declining yields. This improvement was fueled by robust demand during a weekly auction and additional foreign investments. Falling oil prices further enhanced market sentiment. However, traders are keeping a vigilant eye on potential geopolitical tensions and weather disruptions. Furthermore, an upcoming Bloomberg inclusion could alter market dynamics significantly.

articles.title: US stock market today: S&P 500, Nasdaq futures slip after tech-led rally; SK Hynix debut in focus
articles.description: US stock futures dipped on 10 July after a prior rally, but are poised for weekly gains amid easing Middle East tensions and falling oil prices. Investors await SK Hynix's ADR debut as chip stocks lead Wall Street's rise ahead of the approaching earnings season.

articles.description: Wall Street was mixed in muted trading and oil prices swung between small gains and losses after a series of unclaimed airstrikes hit Iran.
